# Registration

## Peachtree Road Race Registration

## AJC Peachtree Road Race

### **Registration for the 56th Running of the Atlanta Journal-Constitution Peachtree Road Race will be open April 1 - 30, 2025.**

**Member registration:** Atlanta Track Club members are guaranteed entry into the race at the lowest price available, and are guaranteed placement in Start Wave A - M.

**Lottery registration:** The lottery system will return in 2025. Non-Atlanta Track Club members must enter through the lottery. All participants that enter will have a fair chance in securing a spot. You must enter a credit card when registering, but your card will not be charged unless you are selected. Lottery notifications will go out in early May. More details to follow.

## **Additional Registration Options**

- **Triple Peach Series**

Sign up for the Atlanta Journal-Constitution Peachtree Road Race, PNC Atlanta 10 Miler, and the Invesco QQQ Thanksgiving Day Half Marathon in registration to lock in the lowest price on these three events. Complete all three races to earn the Triple Peach medal and commemorative item. Commit to your racing goals for the rest of 2025.

- **Peachtree with a Purpose: Kilometer Kids $300 Wave** Participate for Kilometer Kids and receive guaranteed Wave C placement or earlier, a Peachtree Commemorative Medal, an adidas Kilometer Kids bag, a MARTA Breeze pass, and all items including race number shipped via UPS. Demand continues to grow for Kilometer Kids, our free youth running program that in 2024 served 5,000 kids with a game-based curriculum designed to teach kids in grades K-5 about goal setting, healthy habits, respect, and community building through running. $200 tax deduction.

- **Peachtree with a Purpose: Kilometer Kids $500 Wave**

Participate for Kilometer Kids and receive Guaranteed Wave A placement, a Peachtree Commemorative Medal, an adidas Kilometer Kids bag, a MARTA Breeze pass, and all items including race number shipped via UPS. Demand continues to grow for Kilometer Kids, our free youth running program that in 2024 served 5,000 kids with a game-based curriculum designed to teach kids in grades K-5 about goal setting, healthy habits, respect, and community building through running. $420 tax deduction.
